skill.name: looker-studio-bigquery
skill.description: Plan and configure Looker Studio dashboards backed by BigQuery, including data source setup, SQL modeling, dashboard layout, filters, access control, and performance optimization.
skill.summary: Build efficient Looker Studio dashboards using BigQuery data sources, scheduled queries, filters, and access controls.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but semantic review found no executable malware, credential theft, prompt injection, or hidden automation. The confirmed concerns are low severity: documented gcloud commands users may run manually and external reference links to product documentation.

low_findings.title: Manual Cloud CLI Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The only confirmed command pattern is a fenced bash example for creating a Google Cloud project, selecting it, and enabling BigQuery. It is not executed by the skill, but users should review commands before running them because they change cloud resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain explicit gcloud commands in a Markdown code fence. Context shows instructional setup content, not automatic execution or command injection.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Looker Studio, BigQuery, Google developer documentation, and a dashboard design reference. They do not receive local data or credentials in the skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are static Markdown reference links. No network request code, exfiltration path, or dynamic URL construction is present.

low_findings.title: Relative Skill Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal detections are Markdown links to related local skills using relative paths. They are documentation references and no file read, write, delete, or include operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sequences appear only inside Markdown links. There is no code path that consumes these paths for filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Patterns Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits are substrings in prose, SQL examples, metadata, or table labels. No cryptographic function, system probing command, prompt injection, or malicious control text was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual line review found dashboard prose, SQL field names, ordering keywords, and summary text at the flagged locations. The evidence does not show crypto use, host reconnaissance, or instruction override attempts.

content.user_title: Build BigQuery Dashboards in Looker Studio
content.value_statement: BigQuery dashboards can become slow, costly, and hard to share. This skill guides connection setup, SQL modeling, dashboard layout, filters, and performance controls.
actual_capabilities: Guides BigQuery project, dataset, table, API, and IAM setup for reporting workflows.
Explains native Looker Studio connector setup and custom SQL data source options.
Provides dashboard layout guidance for KPIs, trend charts, filters, tables, and maps.
Recommends scheduled queries, partition filters, caching, and chart limits for performance.
Covers sharing, data source permissions, and row-level access control concepts.
Outlines an advanced community connector pattern for custom Looker Studio integrations.
limitations: It does not create dashboards, datasets, or connectors automatically.
Users must have the required Google Cloud and Looker Studio permissions.
Example SQL must be adapted to the user schema, regions, and billing model.
It is not a compliance audit or a replacement for formal data governance review.

use_cases.title: Launch an analytics dashboard
use_cases.description: Create a Looker Studio reporting plan from BigQuery tables with KPIs, trend charts, filters, and share settings.

use_cases.title: Reduce dashboard cost and latency
use_cases.description: Move heavy reports toward scheduled summary tables, partition-aware queries, caching, and controlled chart counts.

use_cases.title: Define stakeholder reporting views
use_cases.description: Translate metrics, date ranges, country filters, and access needs into a clear dashboard specification.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a first dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Looker Studio dashboard using BigQuery. My main metrics are [metrics], and my audience is [audience]. Include data source setup, chart choices, filters, and sharing settings.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare BigQuery data sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my BigQuery reporting need for [business process]. Recommend tables, summary fields, date partitions, scheduled queries, and Looker Studio connector settings.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a slow dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: My Looker Studio dashboard connected to BigQuery is slow and expensive. Analyze this report structure: [describe charts and queries]. Suggest query, caching, table, and layout improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Design secure multi-team reporting
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a secure Looker Studio and BigQuery reporting setup for [teams]. Include IAM roles, row-level access, data source permissions, scheduled tables, and operational checks.

output_examples.input: A marketing team needs a monthly campaign dashboard from BigQuery event data.
output: Dashboard sections for revenue, conversions, campaign trend, channel comparison, and detailed campaign table.
Required filters for date range, country, channel, and campaign name.
Recommended scheduled summary table refreshed nightly to reduce live query cost.

output_examples.input: A dashboard has many charts and takes more than ten seconds to load.
output: Reduce the page to core KPIs, trend charts, and one detail table per view.
Use partition-aligned date filters and precomputed summary tables for repeated calculations.
Split unrelated report sections into separate pages with focused data sources.

output_examples.input: A company needs regional users to see only their own market data.
output: Use BigQuery access controls and row-level policies before exposing data in Looker Studio.
Share dashboards with named users or groups instead of public links.
Test each viewer role with sample accounts before rollout.

best_practices: Start with required metrics, audience, refresh cadence, and access rules before choosing charts.
Use scheduled summary tables and partition filters for large or frequently viewed reports.
Keep each dashboard page focused with a date filter, a few core filters, and limited chart count.
anti_patterns: Connecting Looker Studio directly to large raw event tables for every report view.
Adding many unrelated charts to one page without a clear analysis path.
Sharing dashboards broadly before confirming BigQuery permissions and row-level rules.

faq.question: Does this skill run BigQuery commands for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions and examples that users must review and run in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with custom SQL data sources?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use custom SQL and how to pre-aggregate data for reporting.

faq.question: Does it support performance tuning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers scheduled queries, partition filters, caching, and dashboard complexity limits.

faq.question: Can it design secure dashboards?
faq.answer: It gives access control guidance for BigQuery and Looker Studio, including viewer permissions and row-level concepts.

faq.question: Is it specific to one data schema?
faq.answer: No. The examples use common analytics fields, but users must adapt them to their own tables.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: It is best for analysts, BI engineers, and product teams building dashboards from BigQuery data.
